metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario asserts three user-observable signals: (1) the session does NOT disappear past `readyTTL`, (2) state remains `ready` while idle, and (3) the next user prompt resumes `ready → working → ready` normally. All three are within Gemini CLI + irrlicht's transport.

**agent=yes.** Gemini CLI is a long-lived interactive Node REPL (`node .../bin/gemini`). Once launched it sits in its prompt until the user exits — there is no built-in idle self-exit. A multi-turn session driven with two prompts separated by a sleep is the agent's normal operation, so the agent trivially supports sitting idle then resuming.

**daemon=full.** The adapter ties session lifetime to PID liveness, not to a session-level timer: `Process.Match` uses `commandPattern = bin/gemini` (pgrep -f) and `DiscoverPID` binds the launcher by cwd+cmdline (pid.go). The Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.gemini/tmp}` tailing the per-session `*.jsonl`. The classifier's `readyTTL` only sweeps `ready` sessions whose backing PID is gone; while the gemini launcher process is alive the row stays `ready` indefinitely — exactly the PID-liveness path this scenario exercises (the same architecture as the claudecode reference cell, which is `daemon=full`/0.9). The turn arc is well covered: a user-role message opens the turn (working); the parser settles back to `ready` via the inferred `turn_done` — an assistant `gemini` message that carries final text and opens zero tool calls (parser.go ~205-210). Because this scenario's prompts are single-word replies with `Tool calls: ≤ 0`, the turn_done heuristic fires cleanly with no tool-call ambiguity. Cost/token/model observations are emitted (`applyTokens` → Tokens snapshot + deduped Contribution; `ModelName` from the message `model` field), so the widened observation vector (cost_nonzero, tokens_nonzero, agent) is satisfied.

**driver=ready.** The interactive driver (`replaydata/agents/gemini-cli/driver-interactive.sh`) implements `send`, `slash`, `wait_turn`, and `sleep` as real arms. The recipe needs only `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep`, all live. The stubbed arms (keys, reset_session, restart, sigkill, start_session, …) are not on this recipe's path, so they impose no gap here.
caveats: Gemini emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; the daemon infers turn_done heuristically (assistant message with final text and no open tool calls). This cell's no-tool-call prompts make that inference unambiguous, but the ready-settle is provisional on emission until a live recording confirms it — record promotes it from provisional to settled.

observations.model is intentionally omitted: no prior gemini-cli recording exists to fix the exact normalized model string, and an exact-match model assert would otherwise be a guess. cost_nonzero/tokens_nonzero still run. record should add the observed model.

Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so a ~15-20s idle window cleanly exceeds the TTL; the spec asserts duration_at_least (a floor), so production multi-hour idles are by definition still passing.
Gemini CLI stops serving Google One / unpaid tiers on 2026-06-18; after that, recordings on those tiers fail auth (driver metadata prerequisite).

sources.note: Process.Match = CommandPattern{bin/gemini} + FilesUnderRoot source — session lifetime is tied to PID liveness, not a session timer (the basis for surviving readyTTL).
DiscoverPID binds the launcher by cwd+cmdline; the live launcher process keeps the ready row from being swept.
Inferred turn_done (assistant final text, zero tool calls → settle to ready) at ~L205-210; applyTokens emits Tokens + Contribution; ModelName from the message model field.
send/slash/wait_turn/sleep are real arms (dispatch ~L233-234); the recipe uses only these — multi-session/kill arms are stubbed but off this path.
Reference cell for this scenario (daemon=full/0.9) — same PID-liveness-survives-readyTTL mechanism; recipe templated from its send+wait+20s sleep+send+wait+5s shape.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth-personal); set up out-of-band, no API key
irrlichd running with IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s (default 30 min would force a 32+ min recording)
irrlichd in --record mode with grant-all permission mode (transcripts permission)
fresh cwd: first run shows a trust-folder prompt the driver auto-accepts (gemini -y)
setup: Driver launches the gemini REPL in a fresh cwd; the daemon binds the launcher PID via cwd+cmdline.
The first post-turn sleep (20s) is calibrated to ~1.33x a 15s readyTTL — long enough to prove 'past TTL' without burning a full minute.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the gemini session entering ready after turn 1 completes
no transcript_removed fires for the session during the 20s idle window (not swept while the gemini process is alive)
no state_transition to working or waiting fires during the 20s idle window (state stays ready while idle)
after the second send, events.jsonl shows ready → working → ready for the same session (the long-idle session resumes normally)
cost and tokens are nonzero; agent is gemini-cli
